content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Cornmission
Bev Langston
18 Oakcrest Lane
Ne$rport Beachr CA 92560
Dear Ms. Langston:
OUESTION
Are certain expenses associated with a weekend fishing trip
provided to persons lrho nake contributions to Senator Marian
^B"rg"=o.r con-sidered non-monetary contributions to her carnpaign?
CONCLUSION
A fundraising event held in an individual's home is not a
contribution if the total cost of the event is $5oo or less'
If the cost of the meals, air transportation, and entertainrnent
provi.ded to participants amounts to more than $500, all payroents
ior these itLns will constitute non-monetary contributions to
Senator Bergeson.
FACTS
To help raise campaign funds for Marian Bergeson for Lt'
Governor, you plan to provide a neekend fishing trip to
individuali who contriLute S1,ooo to senator Bergeson's campaign
cornrnittee. Three of these weekend fishing trips have been
planned. Lodging will be provided in a private residence owned by
iive partners, including you and four other fanilies. 
At least
one of the owners will be present. Private air transportation nay
be provided by vari.ous partners of the residence. Use of boats
and fishing equipment wilt also be available.
February 27, ]-99O
Re: Your Request For Advice
Our File No. A-9 0-041
You have requested advice concerning the campaign provisions
of the Political 
Reform Act.1/
l/ 
covernment code Sections 8lOOo-91015. Al1 statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Corunission regulations appear at 2 california Code of Regulations
Section 18ooo, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California code of Regulatj,ons.
-12f1 J Street, Suite Strtr o P.O. Box 8O7 o Srcramento C.A. 95SO+-O8rr7 . (r)16) _122-5660


Bev Langston
Page 2
ANALYSIS
The tenn 'rcontributionrr does not include:
...a pa)rnent made by an occupant of a home or office for
costs related to any Deeting or fundraising event held in
the occupant,s home or office if the costs for the meeting
or fundraising event are five hundred doll-ars ($5oo) or less.
Section 82O15.
If the cost of the meals, air transportation, entertainment
and out of pocket expenses, including any additional equiprnent or
suppties purchased (e.9., fishing equipnrent, bait, etc.) anounts
to more than 9500, aII paynents for these items must be reported
by Senator Bergeson as a non-monetary contribution. 
This includes
air transportation or other items provided by others j,n connection
with the fishing trip.
To determine the value of the air transportation, you should
estimate the fair market value by using the comrnercial air rate,
or, alternatively, the charter rate divided by the nurober of
passengers. (In re Stone (1977) 3 FPPC ops. 52, copy enclosed.)
The Act places linitations 
on contributions to candidates for
elective office based on a fiscal year (July 1 - June 30).
(Sections 85301-85303.) Monetary and non-monetary contributions,
as well as Ioans and enforceable pronises, from a trpersontr to a
candidate are linited to $1,000 per fiscal year. 
(section 85301.)
A irpersonrr includes an individual, corporation, or other entity.
(Section 8s102 (b) . )
If you have additional guestions, please contact ue at (916)
322-5662.
Sincerely,
Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel
By: Alice Hughes
Political 
Reform Consultant
Enclosures
